zh.lifeSummary: 當John "The Elder" Blake於大約1521年出生在英國薩默塞特奧維 斯度維時，他的父親Humphrey Blake29歲，母親Agnes21歲。他於1548年8月18日與Joan Rawson在英國薩默塞特奧維 斯度維結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1576年12月10日在他的家鄉享年56歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特奧維 斯度維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John "The Elder" Blake于大约1521年出生在英国薩默塞特奧維 斯度維时，他的父亲Humphrey Blake29岁，母亲Agnes21岁。他于1548年8月18日与Joan Rawson在英国薩默塞特奧維 斯度維结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1576年12月10日在他的家乡享年56岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特奧維 斯度維。

nameMeanings.name: Blake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (England and central Scotland): variant of Black 1, meaning ‘swarthy’ or ‘dark-haired’, from a byform of the Old English adjective blæc, blac ‘black’, with change of vowel length.

English: nickname from Middle English blak(e) (Old English blāc) ‘wan, pale, white, fair’. In Middle English the two words blac and blāc, with opposite meanings, fell together as Middle English blake. In the absence of independent evidence as to whether the person referred to was dark or fair, it is now impossible to tell which sense was originally meant.
English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English bleik, blaik>, blek(e) (Old Norse bleikr) ‘pale or sallow’ (in complexion).

variants.name: Bleak
meanings: English: mostly a variant of   Blake  , but perhaps also of   Blick  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blaikie
meanings: Scottish: nickname from a pet form of   Blake  .

variants.name: Blakes
meanings: English: variant of Blake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: "The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Elder"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, itself derived from an Old English personal name derived from æðel ‘noble’ + mǣr ‘famous’. This has been used as a given name in the United States since the 19th century, in honour of the brothers Ebenezer and Jonathan Elmer , leading activists in the American Revolution. It is also found in Canada.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When John "The Elder" Blake was born about 1521, in Over Stowey, Somerset, England, his father, Humphrey Blake, was 29 and his mother, Agnes, was 21. He married Joan Rawson on 18 August 1548, in Over Stowey, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 December 1576, in his hometown, at the age of 56, and was buried in Over Stowey, Somerset, England.

Il a épousé Joan Rawson le 18 août 1548, à Over Stowey, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 10 décembre 1576, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Over Stowey, Somerset, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: John "The Elder" Blake(이)가 대략 1521년 영국 서머세트 버 스타우이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Humphrey Blake(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1548년 8월 18일 영국 서머세트 버 스타우이에서 Joan Rawson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1576년 12월 10일 그의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 버 스타우이에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Joan Rawson em 18 de agosto de 1548, em Over Stowey, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда John "The Elder" Blake родился около 1521 г. в местности овир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Humphrey Blake было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Rawson 18 августа 1548 г. в местности овир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 декабря 1576 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности овир стоуи, сомирсет, Англия.
